title: Rebaseline endpoint/duplicate-route ratchets + fix API Tests collection abort (CI-RED-015-K)
intent: Two CI/test-bookkeeping fixes against current main 9c9406f6, both surfaced while rebaselining the CI-RED-015 tail. (1) Endpoint/duplicate-route debt ratchets in test_technical_debt_gates.py drifted red on main independent of any PR (router-consolidation merges); re-declare at exact current count. (2) #163 made app/ci/check_complexity.py sys.exit(2) at import when radon is absent, and added a test importing it; the API Tests job has no radon, so collection aborted with INTERNALERROR (whole job red since #163, confirmed by base-comparison). Make the module import-safe and skip the guard test when radon is absent. No production runtime code, no routes. (CI-RED-015-K body-solver/IBG slice is already green via #158/#160/#161/#163 — no fix needed there.)

diff_articulation.what_changed: (1) test_technical_debt_gates.py: TARGET_MAX_ENDPOINTS 1185->1220, TARGET_MAX_DUPLICATE_ROUTES 123->128 (exact measured current counts). (2) metrics/debt_history.json: append a current-counts snapshot so test_endpoints_not_increasing re-baselines off current reality. (3) app/ci/check_complexity.py: on radon ImportError set cc_visit=None instead of sys.exit(2), so importing the module never exits; defer the missing-radon failure to check_complexity()/main() (raise / return 2). (4) test_complexity_key_collision.py: pytest.importorskip('radon') before importing the gate (defense if radon is ever absent). (5) core_ci.yml: install radon in the api-tests job so that guard test actually runs in CI (radon stays out of requirements.txt to keep the deployed image lean; the geometry-parity job runs no pytest and is unchanged).
diff_articulation.why_not_redundant: The ratchet reds and the INTERNALERROR are both pre-existing main-state drift/regressions (this branch makes no app/route change); confirmed via base-comparison that main's API Tests aborts identically. Re-baselining is the gate's designed maintenance path. The import-safety fix is root-cause: a CI-script module must never sys.exit at import or any importer (here a test) kills the whole pytest collection in an environment lacking radon.

commands_run: narrow body-solver/IBG witness (dev-order primary target): 15 passed -> slice already green, no fix needed
body-solver route-mount witness: all four /api/body/... routes mounted
measured current debt counts: endpoints=1220, duplicate=128, large_files=99, god_objects=15, bare_except=6
radon-absent simulation: import OK (cc_visit=None), main() returns 2 (no SystemExit at import)
test_complexity_key_collision.py with radon present: 2 passed; complexity gate: OK exit 0
base-comparison: main 9c9406f6/fcf10905 API Tests INTERNALERROR identical (check_complexity.py radon import) -> pre-existing regression from #163
full suite witnessed by CI (7484 serial tests impractical locally)

files.intent: Rebaseline TARGET_MAX_ENDPOINTS 1185->1220 and TARGET_MAX_DUPLICATE_ROUTES 123->128 to current measured counts
Append current-counts snapshot so the no-increase ratchet re-baselines off current reality
Make the module import-safe when radon is absent (cc_visit=None) instead of sys.exit(2) at import; defer the failure to use
files.behavior_change: none - CLI/gate behavior with radon present is unchanged; only the no-radon import path changes from process-exit to graceful deferral

files.intent: Skip the guard test when radon is absent (pytest.importorskip) so the API Tests job collects
Install radon in the api-tests job so the complexity-key collision guard test actually runs in CI (radon kept out of requirements.txt to keep the deployed image lean)
files.behavior_change: none - CI dependency install only (api-tests job; geometry-parity job unaffected, it runs no pytest)
